description: Official client SDKs for the OpenDPP Digital Product Passport API — fully-typed clients generated from the public OpenAPI contract and version-locked to it. TypeScript (@opendpp/sdk) is live; Python (opendpp) is planned.
rules: Install the SDK major that matches the `/api/v1` contract major you call. The package version IS the OpenAPI contract version — `@opendpp/sdk@1.6.0` targets contract `1.6.0`.

Create a client with `createOpenDppClient({ apiKey })` and pass it per call (`getPassport({ client, path: { id } })`), or configure the exported default `client` globally via `client.setConfig({ baseUrl, auth })`.

`baseUrl` defaults to the public hosted node `https://opendpp-node.eu`. The API key is sent as `Authorization: Bearer <key>` only on operations that declare a security requirement — public operations like `getHealth` need no key.

Every endpoint is a tree-shakeable, fully-typed function (`createPassport`, `getPassport`, `sealPassport`, `decodeGs1`, `resolveGs1Gtin`, `whoami`, …) and every request/response shape is an exported type. Import named operations from the package root; do not reach into `src/generated`.
The TypeScript SDK is ESM-only, targets Node >= 20, and has zero runtime dependencies (the fetch client is bundled).
The SDK is ergonomics only — it embeds no tier/masking logic and no restricted-key knowledge. All privileged behavior lives in the hosted node behind a Developer-Plan key.
